items.title: Leica Biosystems Announces Partnership With Molecular Instruments As HCR™ Pro RNA-ISH Technology Is Released On The BOND RX and BOND RX
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: Molecular Instruments HCR Pro now on Leica BOND RX automation; direct RNAscope threat on speed, multiplexing, and lab workflow integration. Field must know.

impact_brief.what_happened: Molecular Instruments has partnered with Leica Biosystems to launch HCR Pro (its hybridization chain reaction ISH technology) on the BOND RX automated platform. This integrates a direct RNAscope competitor into one of the most widely deployed in situ hybridization instruments in pathology labs, combining HCR's chemistry with Leica's installed base and automation.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: This is a meaningful ecosystem play that removes a friction point for HCR adoption. Labs already using BOND RX can now access an alternative to RNAscope without workflow disruption or capital investment in new hardware. Leica's automation, established service relationships, and pathology credibility make HCR Pro a credible in-lab alternative rather than a specialty tool. ACD's primary vulnerability is labs that have standardized on Leica instruments and value workflow simplicity over chemistry optionality.
at_risk: Leica-dependent labs may perceive HCR Pro as 'good enough' and bundled, lowering willingness to adopt RNAscope as a parallel or replacement chemistry.
Leica's commercial influence and bundling incentives could favor HCR Pro in marketing and training within its installed base.
Speed and automation claims around HCR may appeal to high-throughput clinical labs that prioritize turnaround time over multiplexing depth or single-molecule validation.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and specificity remain chemistry advantages that automation alone cannot replicate; HCR chemistry trade-offs (kinetics, multiplexing ceiling) still apply.
RNAscope's platform agnosticity (not locked to Leica or any single vendor) is strategically valuable as labs increasingly demand flexibility and avoid single-vendor dependency.
RNAscope's deep, published validation across tissue types and disease states in both research and clinical settings is a credibility moat that a new Leica integration does not displace.

items.title: Biocare Medical and Molecular Instruments Partner to Revolutionize Automated Bioimaging Through ONCORE Pro X and HCR™ RNA-ISH Assays
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: Molecular Instruments + Biocare automation partnership scales HCR RNA-ISH head-to-head against RNAscope; threatens workflow adoption at core ISH labs.

impact_brief.what_happened: Molecular Instruments and Biocare Medical announced a partnership integrating MI's HCR RNA-ISH assay onto Biocare's ONCORE Pro X automated platform. The collaboration aims to streamline HCR workflows for academic, pharma, and clinical labs by combining MI's room-temperature isothermal amplification chemistry with Biocare's open, versatile automation system.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: This is a direct threat to RNAscope's position in automated ISH labs. HCR has historically been a niche competitor, but pairing it with a trusted, platform-agnostic automation vendor (Biocare) removes friction from adoption and positions HCR as a turnkey alternative for labs already considering or using ONCORE systems. The emphasis on 'mild sample prep, no protease digestion' and 'room-temperature amplification' directly counters RNAscope's perceived complexity and requirement for more aggressive tissue handling.
at_risk: Customer consideration at labs with existing or planned ONCORE Pro X installations, where HCR now becomes a native, validated workflow rather than a bolt-on ISH option.
RNAscope's platform-agnostic messaging loses force if HCR becomes the de facto ISH standard on Biocare's instrument ecosystem and workflow ecosystem.
Potential loss of assay bundling and sample throughput to HCR at sites where automation and ease-of-use are primary decision drivers, not sensitivity or tissue coverage.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and deep, multi-tissue validation remain unmatched; HCR's isothermal chemistry and morphology claims have not been validated at the scale or breadth of RNAscope's published data.

Biocare's 'open' automation does not eliminate customer lock-in risk if HCR assays and Biocare reagents become the path of least resistance, but RNAscope's compatibility with multiple instruments (including Biocare) preserves optionality for labs already invested in our workflow.

HCR's room-temperature workflow and simpler prep are marketing advantages, but they do not address the specific applications where RNAscope's single-molecule resolution and multiplexing depth are non-negotiable (e.g., low-abundance targets, dense multiplex in clinical samples).

items.title: Court Finds Advanced Cell Diagnostics Patents Invalid in Lawsuit with Molecular Instruments
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: Patent invalidation ruling favors Molecular Instruments HCR; direct threat to ACD IP moat and RNAscope market defensibility.

impact_brief.what_happened: A court has invalidated patents held by Advanced Cell Diagnostics in litigation with Molecular Instruments, the company behind HCR (Hybridization Chain Reaction) in situ hybridization technology. This ruling removes at least one layer of IP protection that ACD had relied on to defend RNAscope's market position against HCR as a direct competitor.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: ACD's competitive advantage has rested partly on patent coverage of RNAscope's single-molecule detection and specificity claims. Loss of patent validity weakens ACD's ability to deter or block Molecular Instruments' market expansion with HCR, a technically comparable platform that is already gaining traction in spatial biology and research applications. This opens the door for more aggressive competition on product merit and pricing rather than IP exclusivity.
at_risk: Market share in in situ hybridization applications where HCR and RNAscope directly compete, particularly in research and emerging clinical diagnostics
Negotiating leverage with potential customers and OEM partners who may now perceive lower switching costs or IP risk when evaluating HCR as an alternative
Ability to command premium pricing if RNAscope's defensibility is seen as compromised
holding_ground: RNAscope's proven track record of single-molecule sensitivity and extensive validation across tissue types and disease states, which remains independent of patent status
Platform-agnostic flexibility and lack of instrument lock-in, which differentiates ACD from bundled competitors and may appeal to customers skeptical of ecosystem dependence
Remaining patent portfolio and potential for design-around improvements that do not infringe invalidated claims

items.title: Molecular Instruments Launches HCR™ Pro RNA-ISH for Manual Workflows, Enabling Extreme-Sensitivity RNA Imaging in an Accessible Format
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: Molecular Instruments launches HCR Pro for manual RNA-ISH workflows with extreme sensitivity. Direct RNAscope competitor expanding accessible ISH reach. Field alert required.

impact_brief.what_happened: Molecular Instruments launched HCR Pro, a manual workflow version of their HCR (Hybridization Chain Reaction) RNA in situ hybridization product designed for extreme sensitivity imaging. This expands HCR beyond automated platforms into the hands-on lab bench space where RNAscope has historically held strong adoption.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: Molecular Instruments is directly targeting RNAscope's core installed base by making HCR accessible to labs that prefer or require manual ISH workflows. If HCR Pro delivers on sensitivity claims without requiring proprietary instruments, it removes a key barrier to adoption and threatens RNAscope's position in research and clinical labs that value platform independence.
at_risk: Customer switching risk in labs currently using RNAscope for manual multiplexed ISH, especially if HCR Pro demonstrates superior sensitivity or lower reagent cost per sample.
Perception that HCR technology may be closing the sensitivity gap that has been a RNAscope differentiator.
holding_ground: RNAscope's deep, multi-year validation across diverse tissue types and disease models remains a competitive moat that a new product launch cannot instantly replicate.
Single-molecule resolution and specificity track record is established; HCR Pro must prove equivalent performance across the same breadth of applications.
RNAscope's ecosystem flexibility and lack of instrument lock-in remain a genuine advantage if customers perceive HCR as tied to Molecular Instruments' assay chemistry or support model.

items.title: Molecular Instruments Announces Fully Ambient-Temperature HCR™ Pro RNA-ISH on the BOND-III, Setting a New Standard for Clinical Automation
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: Molecular Instruments launches HCR Pro on BOND-III automation at ambient temperature, directly competing with RNAscope speed and accessibility in clinical labs.

impact_brief.what_happened: Molecular Instruments announced HCR Pro, their in situ hybridization method, is now compatible with the Leica BOND-III automated platform and runs at ambient temperature without heating steps. This positions HCR as a turnkey clinical automation solution competing directly with RNAscope's ease of use and workflow speed.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: HCR has long been a head-to-head ISH rival to RNAscope on technical merit, but lacked the clinical automation pedigree and standardized workflow that make RNAscope attractive in busy pathology labs. BOND-III integration removes a key friction point and signals Molecular Instruments is targeting the same clinical and research labs where ACD has established adoption. Ambient-temperature chemistry is also a practical win for lab operations.
at_risk: Clinical labs evaluating ISH platforms may now view HCR Pro as a credible alternative to RNAscope, especially if they already own or prefer BOND-III infrastructure.
New customer acquisition in clinical settings where workflow automation and minimal hands-on time are deciding factors.
Perception that RNAscope requires more technical expertise or workflow integration compared to a fully automated, temperature-controlled assay.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and multiplexing specificity remain unmatched and validated across a deeper library of tissue and disease contexts.
ACD's platform-agnostic design means RNAscope works on BOND-III and other automation systems too, giving customers flexibility rather than lock-in to one vendor's workflow.
RNAscope's established customer base and extensive published validation in clinical and research settings create switching friction.

items.title: Molecular Instruments and Visiopharm Announce Strategic Partnership to Combine AI-Driven Precision Pathology with HCR™ Pro Assays to Advance Clinical-Grade RNA-ISH
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: MI + Visiopharm AI-pathology integration for HCR Pro clinical RNA-ISH poses direct RNAscope threat in clinical/translational workflows.

impact_brief.what_happened: Molecular Instruments (HCR) has partnered with Visiopharm to bundle HCR Pro RNA-ISH assays with Visiopharm's Oncotopix Discovery AI-driven image analysis software. The partnership emphasizes clinical-grade performance, protease-free prep, and AI-powered segmentation and spot counting for both chromogenic and fluorescent detection, including multiplex RNA-ISH + IHC/IF workflows.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: This is a direct competitive move against RNAscope in the clinical pathology and translational research space. By pairing HCR's chemistry with an established AI pathology software platform, MI is addressing a known workflow pain point (image analysis and quantification) and positioning HCR Pro as a complete, clinical-ready solution. The emphasis on protease-free prep, sensitivity, specificity, and seamless IHC/IF integration mirrors RNAscope's core value propositions, and the AI layer aims to reduce adoption friction in clinical labs.
at_risk: Clinical pathology and diagnostic lab adoption where turnkey workflows and validated software pipelines drive purchase decisions and lock-in.
Translational research accounts that prioritize integrated solutions and AI-assisted image analysis over assay flexibility, particularly in oncology and complex tissue applications.
Accounts currently using RNAscope with third-party image analysis tools may view this bundle as a more streamlined, validated alternative with lower implementation risk.
holding_ground: RNAscope's established single-molecule sensitivity, specificity, and deep validation across diverse tissue types and disease contexts remain unmatched and independent of vendor image analysis tools.
Platform agnosticity remains a strategic differentiator; customers can use RNAscope with any imaging system and analysis software, avoiding vendor lock-in that the MI-Visiopharm bundle may impose.

RNAscope's installed base, workflow familiarity, and proven clinical validation history in pathology labs provide inertia that a new bundled offering must overcome with clear, quantified superiority claims.

items.title: Molecular Instruments Launches the HCR™ Gold and HCR™ Pro Product Lines to Significantly Expand the HCR™ Imaging Platform
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: Molecular Instruments expands HCR platform with Gold/Pro lines; direct RNAscope competitor repositioning on speed, multiplexing, and price.

impact_brief.what_happened: Molecular Instruments has launched HCR Gold and HCR Pro product lines to expand its Hybridization Chain Reaction (HCR) platform. This move signals the company is positioning HCR as a broader competitive alternative across speed, multiplexing capacity, and pricing tiers, directly targeting the use cases and customer segments where RNAscope holds significant share.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: HCR has been a nascent but growing threat in ISH; this product line expansion suggests Molecular Instruments is maturing its go-to-market and may be investing in sales and validation infrastructure to compete more directly with ACD. The introduction of Gold and Pro tiers implies product differentiation and price segmentation that could appeal to customers who view RNAscope as costly or inflexible for certain workflows (e.g., rapid turnaround, ultra-high plex).
at_risk: RNAscope's cost perception relative to HCR, especially if Molecular Instruments prices Gold or Pro lines aggressively or bundles them with instruments or services.
Customers seeking faster assay turnaround times or simplified multiplexing workflows; HCR's chemistry and marketing narrative emphasize speed and ease compared to RNAscope.
Market share in emerging application areas (e.g., clinical diagnostics, ultra-high plex panels) where speed and cost are primary drivers and validation maturity is still building.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and deep, cross-tissue validation remain unmatched and are differentiators in applications requiring high confidence (precision diagnostics, complex tissues).

Platform agnosticity and lack of instrument lock-in continue to appeal to labs and clinicians who want flexibility and vendor independence; HCR's success still depends on instrument partnerships or ease of integration.
Established customer base, integration into clinical workflows, and decades of co-citation in high-impact literature are switching costs that a new product line alone cannot easily overcome.

items.title: Molecular Instruments and Abcam Announce Broad Compatibility of the HCR™ HiFi Encoder with Abcam’s Best-in-Class RabMAb® and Mouse Antibody Portfolio
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: Molecular Instruments expands HCR HiFi encoder compatibility with Abcam antibodies, lowering barrier to adoption against RNAscope.

impact_brief.what_happened: Molecular Instruments announced that its HCR HiFi Encoder is now compatible with Abcam's broad portfolio of RabMAb and mouse antibodies. This partnership reduces friction for customers wanting to adopt HCR by allowing them to use existing, validated antibodies without needing new reagents or workflows.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: HCR is RNAscope's most direct technical competitor in the single-molecule ISH space. Lowering the practical barrier to HCR adoption by leveraging Abcam's established antibody portfolio makes it easier for labs already invested in Abcam reagents to switch, especially if they view HCR as a simpler or more cost-effective alternative to RNAscope.
at_risk: Customer switching: labs using Abcam antibodies may see HCR as a lower-friction on-ramp, particularly in cost-sensitive or resource-limited settings
Workflow bundling: compatibility deals like this create ecosystem pressure and make it harder for RNAscope to be the default choice in multiplex ISH projects
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and specificity remain technically differentiated and are backed by deeper validation across tissue types and disease states

Platform agnosticity is a genuine advantage: RNAscope works with any antibody and does not lock customers into one vendor's ecosystem, whereas HCR partnerships create dependencies on specific reagent suppliers

items.title: A spatial atlas of the healthy human liver redefines mammalian liver zonation
items.summary: Yakubovsky et al., Nature, 2026 A new spatial transcriptomics atlas of the healthy human liver, published in Nature by Yakubovsky and colleagues at the Weizmann Institute of Science with collaborators at Sheba Medical Center and... The post A spatial atlas of the healthy human liver redefines mammalian liver zonation appeared first on Vizgen .
items.why: Vizgen publishes landmark Nature liver spatial atlas. Demonstrates MERSCOPE capability in human tissue; validates competitive platform momentum. Field must prepare defenses.

impact_brief.what_happened: Vizgen published a major Nature paper on human liver zonation using their MERSCOPE platform with MERFISH, demonstrating single-molecule, single-cell spatial validation of transcriptomics-based zonation patterns. The work involved live healthy donor tissue and was conducted by Weizmann Institute researchers with collaborators at Mayo Clinic and Sheba Medical Center, establishing a high-quality reference atlas that challenges prior mouse-based assumptions about liver biology.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: This is a high-profile validation of Vizgen's MERSCOPE platform in a landmark translational biology context, positioning MERFISH on MERSCOPE as a credible orthogonal method for spatial validation at single-molecule, single-cell resolution. The Nature publication, institutional pedigree, and clinical relevance amplify Vizgen's platform authority in the spatial transcriptomics market and may accelerate adoption among large academic medical centers and pharma partners seeking validated spatial workflows.
at_risk: Customer perception that MERSCOPE is the preferred or only platform for high-confidence spatial validation in human tissue, especially for translational work where Nature-publication-grade rigor is expected.
Potential bundling effect: Mayo Clinic and other major medical centers may adopt Vizgen more broadly if MERSCOPE becomes the standard-of-care validation tool in their atlasing pipelines.
RNAscope's positioning as an orthogonal, open-platform validation method is relative; Vizgen can credibly claim integrated, proprietary workflow superiority in translational contexts.
holding_ground: RNAscope remains platform-agnostic and does not lock customers into one vendor's instruments, preserving flexibility for labs that need to work across multiple spatial and sequencing technologies.
Single-molecule sensitivity and specificity are foundational to RNAscope's value; this Vizgen paper validates the market need for single-molecule validation, not Vizgen's unique ownership of it.

ACD's deep tissue validation library and established use in adjacent-normal and post-mortem tissue give RNAscope credibility in the exact sample types this atlas rejected as confounded, positioning RNAscope as a pragmatic choice for real-world tissue sources.

items.title: Indica Labs and Molecular Instruments Announce Partnership to Advance AI-Powered Quantitative Image Analysis with HCR™ RNA-ISH
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: Molecular Instruments strengthens HCR competitive moat via Indica Labs (HALO) AI integration; direct RNAscope threat on analysis workflow.

impact_brief.what_happened: Molecular Instruments (MI) and Indica Labs have announced a partnership to integrate HCR RNA-ISH staining with Indica's HALO AI-powered image analysis software. The partnership bundles MI's HCR chemistry (which emphasizes mild sample prep and morphology preservation) with downstream AI quantification and spatial analysis, creating a turnkey ISH-to-insight workflow.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: This move directly targets RNAscope's historical advantage in analysis agnosticism. By co-marketing HCR with HALO, MI creates ecosystem lock-in similar to what larger platforms (10x, Bruker) use: customers adopt the chemistry partly because the analysis is already optimized for it. The emphasis on AI-powered quantification and spatial relationships also positions HCR against RNAscope's core use cases in gene expression mapping and cell phenotyping.
at_risk: RNAscope's appeal to labs that value flexibility and want to choose their analysis tool independently, since HCR+HALO now presents a pre-validated, easier integrated option.
RNAscope's competitive position in research labs and pharma that already use HALO or are considering it, where switching to HCR for ISH becomes lower friction.
Customer perception that RNAscope requires more legwork post-acquisition, since HALO modules are described as pre-built and not requiring algorithm design from scratch.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and long track record of validation across diverse tissue types remain unmatched and critical for high-resolution spatial biology applications.

RNAscope's platform-agnostic design means it works with any imaging system and any analysis software (HALO included), so labs are not forced to choose; RNAscope can be paired with HALO downstream independently.

HCR's claimed advantage (no protease, preserved morphology) is a chemistry claim, not a guarantee of better biology or analysis output; RNAscope's specificity and sensitivity remain the reference standard in many applications.

items.title: Vizgen Launches New Panels and AI Segmentation Tools to Advance Spatial Biology Research
items.summary: Pre-designed and Add-on gene panels are a new means of accessing Vizgen’s validation and customization capabilities Extended range of cell segmentation models reflective of ongoing initiative to streamline advanced downstream analysis Waltham, Mass. – April... The post Vizgen Launches New Panels and AI Segmentation Tools to Advance Spatial Biology Research appeared first on Vizgen .
items.why: Vizgen expands MERSCOPE with pre-designed panels and AI segmentation. Direct threat to RNAscope multiplexing and ease-of-use positioning. Field must know immediately.

impact_brief.what_happened: Vizgen announced pre-designed gene panels for MERSCOPE/MERSCOPE Ultra, customizable add-on panels (up to 100 genes), and integrated AI segmentation tools (Cellpose-SAM and InstanSeg) in their post-processing platform. The move aims to lower adoption friction and streamline the end-to-end workflow from panel design through data analysis.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: Vizgen is directly addressing workflow friction points that RNAscope has historically exploited as competitive advantages: ease of use, panel flexibility, and integrated analysis. By bundling curated panels with open-source segmentation plugins, Vizgen strengthens lock-in to MERSCOPE hardware while making the platform more accessible to new users. This compresses the gap between 'out-of-the-box simplicity' and 'custom flexibility', a positioning space where RNAscope has gained traction.
at_risk: RNAscope's relative advantage in panel customization and rapid deployment for non-standard applications if Vizgen's add-on capability (up to 100 genes) proves fast and reliable.
Customer perception that MERSCOPE now offers turnkey simplicity without sacrificing flexibility, potentially shifting the 'ease vs. power' trade-off narrative.
RNAscope's platform-agnostic positioning may feel less compelling if MERSCOPE becomes a fully integrated, end-to-end solution with pre-validated workflows and native bioinformatics.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and specificity remain unmatched; pre-designed panels and better UX do not solve detection limits or cross-talk in high-plex applications.
RNAscope works on any imaging platform (Zeiss, Nikon, etc.) without hardware lock-in; MERSCOPE customers remain dependent on Vizgen instruments and reagent supply.
RNAscope's deep validation across diverse tissue types and disease models is a multi-year moat that panel curation alone cannot replicate.

items.title: Vizgen® to Advance Cell Atlases with New Volumetric Tissue Mapping Spatial Data and Services at HCA 2026 Meeting
items.summary: Company plans to showcase new volumetric MERFISH datasets in mouse brain and human neurodegenerative brain tissue, generated on the existing MERSCOPE Ultra™ Platform WALTHAM, Mass. – June 8, 2026 – Vizgen, Inc., a leader in spatial multiomics and developer of the MERSCOPE Ultra™ Platform, today... The post Vizgen® to Advance Cell Atlases with New Volumetric Tissue Mapping Spatial Data and Services at HCA 2026 Meeting appeared first on Vizgen .
items.why: Vizgen launching volumetric MERFISH datasets in brain tissue at HCA 2026; MERSCOPE Ultra advancing against RNAscope in neurodegen spatial profiling. Monitor adoption.

impact_brief.what_happened: Vizgen announced volumetric MERFISH datasets and Volumetric Tissue Mapping services, showcasing 3D spatial transcriptomics data in mouse and human neurodegenerative brain tissue on the MERSCOPE Ultra platform. The company is also launching a Cell Atlasing Hub and offering project-based data generation services to the cell atlasing community.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: This move positions Vizgen to capture high-value neurodegenerative and brain atlasing workflows by solving a real limitation of 2D ISH methods: loss of spatial context in thick tissue. If volumetric MERFISH becomes the standard for brain and CNS cell atlasing, ACD risks losing design-in opportunities in a large, well-funded research segment where RNAscope currently competes on validation and flexibility.
at_risk: Neurodegenerative and brain tissue atlasing programs may prefer Vizgen's native 3D capability over RNAscope's single-plane readout, especially if volumetric datasets become a publication norm at HCA and similar consortia.

Vizgen's bundled services model (instrument, chemistry, and data generation) may reduce customer friction in adopting volumetric workflows compared to integrating RNAscope into a custom 3D imaging pipeline.
MERSCOPE Ultra's established user base in spatial transcriptomics and high-profile HCA partnerships give Vizgen strong credibility to anchor the volumetric brain atlasing narrative.
holding_ground: RNAscope's platform-agnostic design and single-molecule sensitivity remain advantageous for hybrid workflows or labs that want to combine ISH with other imaging modalities without vendor lock-in.

ACD's broad tissue validation (not just brain) and proven multiplexing on existing customer instruments retain relevance for users who need flexibility or who are already embedded in non-Vizgen imaging systems.

Volumetric MERFISH requires custom thick-tissue protocols and imaging infrastructure; RNAscope's ease of use on standard histology workflows and existing microscopes may retain value in labs without volumetric aspirations or in tissues where 2D atlasing remains sufficient.

items.title: Molecular Instruments® Continues RNA Imaging Leadership with Next-Generation miRNA/siRNA Detection and Clinical-Grade Ready-to-Use Kits
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: MI launches clinical-grade miRNA/siRNA kits; direct RNAscope threat on speed, ease, and non-coding RNA capability.

impact_brief.what_happened: Molecular Instruments announced clinical-grade ready-to-use kits for miRNA and siRNA detection, positioning their HCR platform as turnkey for non-coding RNA work. The emphasis on clinical readiness and ease of use signals a move beyond research-grade workflows into higher-friction customer segments.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: This targets a specific RNAscope vulnerability: non-coding RNA detection requires custom probe design, and the ready-to-use format removes a major friction point for labs seeking plug-and-play miRNA workflows. If these kits are genuinely clinical-grade validated, MI gains credibility with diagnostic and clinical research labs that value standardization and regulatory pathway clarity.
at_risk: Clinical and diagnostic labs evaluating miRNA panels may default to MI's turnkey offering over RNAscope's custom-probe model, especially if regulatory documentation is already in place.
Speed to result and hands-on complexity favor MI's ready-to-use format in time-constrained clinical labs and core facilities with limited assay development bandwidth.
Non-coding RNA is a growing segment; RNAscope's strength in mRNA should not be assumed to carry into miRNA/siRNA competitive positioning.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and multiplexing depth on mRNA remain unmatched for discovery and research; clinical kits alone do not invalidate platform superiority for complex tissue analysis.
RNAscope's platform-agnostic flexibility and compatibility with multiple imaging systems and tissue types prevent lock-in and retain value for labs with existing infrastructure investments.

MI's clinical-grade launch is credible but unproven at scale; RNAscope has deep validation libraries and established adoption in pathology and spatial biology workflows that clinical kits do not immediately displace.

items.title: HistoWiz and Molecular Instruments® Collaborate to Advance ISH Services powered by HCR™ RNA-ISH
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: Molecular Instruments expands HCR RNA-ISH distribution through HistoWiz service partnership; direct threat to RNAscope lab adoption and outsourcing revenue streams.

impact_brief.what_happened: Molecular Instruments and HistoWiz announced a co-marketing and service partnership in which HistoWiz becomes a qualified service provider for HCR RNA-ISH assays. HistoWiz will integrate MI's HCR technology into its histology and pathology services, and MI will promote HistoWiz to its customer base. Both companies will pursue joint research in immuno-oncology.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: This partnership directly expands HCR's reach into the outsourced ISH services market, where HistoWiz's established relationships with biopharmaceutical and academic customers create a new distribution channel for a direct RNAscope competitor. By bundling HCR into a full-service histology and pathology workflow with slide processing, staining, imaging, and analysis, MI and HistoWiz lower friction for customers to adopt HCR over single-reagent ISH platforms. The joint research focus on immuno-oncology and multi-omic workflows signals intent to build clinical and translational credibility in areas where RNAscope is entrenched.
at_risk: Outsourced ISH service revenue from HistoWiz customers and similar service providers considering HCR as an integrated workflow solution.
Lab adoption in biopharmaceutical and academic settings where customers value integrated service bundles and may see HCR's protease-free workflow as operationally simpler than RNAscope.
Credibility in multiplexing and multi-omic (RNA + protein) applications if HCR's clinical-grade validation and HistoWiz's pre-validation work gain market traction in immuno-oncology.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and deep historical validation across tissue types remain unmatched; HistoWiz's integration of HCR does not erase performance gaps in demanding research contexts.
Platform agnosticity is an advantage: RNAscope is not tied to HistoWiz or any single service provider, so customers retain choice and are not locked into a bundled ecosystem.

ACD's direct relationships with labs, reagent-level optimization, and scientific reputation for rigor give us leverage to defend existing accounts and credibly counter claims of HCR simplicity or superiority.

items.title: Molecular Instruments and Standard BioTools Announce Collaboration to Bring Amplified Multi-Omic Readouts to Imaging Mass Cytometry
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: MI partners with Standard BioTools (Hyperion IMC) to integrate HCR RNA-FISH with spatial proteomics, expanding competitive threat beyond RNA into multiplexed tissue imaging.

impact_brief.what_happened: Molecular Instruments announced a collaboration with Standard BioTools to integrate HCR (hybridization chain reaction) RNA-FISH with imaging mass cytometry (IMC), combining RNA and protein detection in a single multiplexed spatial workflow. This moves HCR beyond RNA-only assays into direct competition with multi-omic tissue imaging platforms.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: This partnership strengthens HCR as an end-to-end spatial biology solution and positions it as a direct alternative to RNAscope-based workflows, particularly for customers seeking integrated RNA and protein readouts. Standard BioTools' installed base and clinical validation (Hyperion) gives MI access to established customers and credibility in regulated settings. The collaboration reduces switching costs for customers already on IMC by embedding HCR natively into their workflow.
at_risk: Customers evaluating multi-omic spatial imaging who see HCR + IMC as a simpler, integrated path versus RNAscope plus separate protein detection
Existing Standard BioTools IMC users, who now have a supported RNA option without leaving the ecosystem
Mid-to-high plex RNA workflows where HCR's amplification advantage in signal-to-noise may appeal to labs frustrated with RNAscope background
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule resolution and established validation across diverse tissues remain unmatched by HCR in absolute sensitivity and specificity
RNAscope's platform agnosticity means customers are not forced into a single instrument vendor, whereas HCR + IMC is now a bundled system
Deep, multi-year adoption and publication record with RNAscope in clinical and research labs creates switching inertia that a new partnership must overcome

items.title: Molecular Instruments and Alpenglow Biosciences Partner to Advance 3D RNA Imaging in Intact Biological Samples
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: Molecular Instruments (HCR RNA-FISH) deepens 3D spatial imaging capability via Alpenglow partnership; direct competitive threat to RNAscope multiplexing and tissue-imaging workflows.

impact_brief.what_happened: Molecular Instruments, maker of HCR (Hybridization Chain Reaction) RNA-FISH, has partnered with Alpenglow Biosciences to advance 3D RNA imaging in intact tissues. This move signals HCR is pushing deeper into volumetric and spatial imaging, a core strength of RNAscope.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: HCR has historically been positioned as a cost-effective, open-platform alternative to RNAscope but with lower multiplexing capacity and less clinical/validated adoption. This partnership suggests Molecular Instruments is addressing HCR's volumetric imaging gap, making it a more complete competitor for tissue-based spatial biology workflows where RNAscope currently dominates.
at_risk: RNAscope's advantage in 3D/volumetric multiplexed imaging if Alpenglow's technology materially improves HCR's depth-of-field and Z-stack performance in thick tissues.
Academic and pharma customers evaluating HCR as an open-platform alternative may now see fewer trade-offs between cost savings and spatial imaging capability.
Pipeline and pricing pressure in multiplexed tissue imaging workflows where customers have historically chosen RNAscope for volumetric confidence but may now test HCR as lower-cost option.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and established validation library across diverse tissue types and disease models remain significantly deeper than HCR's.
RNAscope's platform agnosticity and lack of instrument lock-in remain a competitive advantage; HCR+Alpenglow still require evaluation against ACD's flexibility with microscopy hardware.

RNAscope's clinical and translational footprint is broader; this partnership does not immediately shift HCR's presence in regulated diagnostics or GLP workflows where ACD has stronger institutional traction.

items.why: Vizgen expanding MERSCOPE Ultra to organoids; direct threat to RNAscope validation workflows in 3D models. Monitor AGBT presentation for capability claims.

impact_brief.what_happened: Vizgen announced a spatial multiomics roadmap centered on expanding MERSCOPE Ultra for organoid profiling, with new capabilities including modular gene customization (MERSCOPE Add-On Genes), optimized slide chemistry, cloud-based image analysis, and progress toward 3D thick-tissue imaging. The company is positioning itself to capitalize on the FDA and NIH push toward New Approach Methodologies and lab-grown tissue models in drug discovery.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: Vizgen is systematically closing gaps in workflow flexibility and sample-type coverage that have historically differentiated RNAscope. The organoid focus targets a high-value, rapidly growing segment where regulatory bodies and pharma are shifting R&D spend. Vizgen's modular panel customization and cloud analytics also address researcher demands for hypothesis-driven flexibility without losing platform lock-in benefits, which could erode RNAscope's appeal as the 'instrument-agnostic' alternative.
at_risk: RNAscope's positioning as the flexible, non-bundled choice becomes less distinct if Vizgen's MERSCOPE Add-On Genes and cloud platform make MERSCOPE itself feel modular and open.

Organoid and 3D tissue validation is a white-space growth vector for both companies; Vizgen's dedicated focus and FDA/NIH tailwind could establish MERSCOPE as the standard for NAM-driven drug discovery workflows before RNAscope builds comparable literature.
Cloud-based analysis and segmentation may lower barriers to MERSCOPE adoption for sites without deep bioinformatics expertise, expanding Vizgen's addressable market beyond early-adopter labs.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and species-agnostic chemistry remain unmatched for difficult tissues and multiplexing depth; Vizgen's roadmap does not yet claim parity on these core capabilities.

RNAscope has deep, peer-reviewed validation across primary tissues, fixed tissues, and diverse models; organoid validation, while growing, is still a subset of the spatial biology universe, and switching costs are high in established workflows.

If Vizgen's 3D imaging and segmentation claims require proprietary MERSCOPE hardware or closed workflows, RNAscope's compatibility with third-party imaging systems (Zeiss, Leica, etc.) remains a genuine value for labs seeking true independence and cost control.

items.title: Molecular Instruments® RNA In Situ Hybridization (RNA-ISH) Technology Enables Breakthrough Laboratory-Developed Tests (LDTs) to Advance Precision Medicine for Inflammatory Skin Disease
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: MI pivoting HCR RNA-ISH into clinical LDTs for inflammatory skin disease; direct RNAscope competitive threat in precision diagnostics.

impact_brief.what_happened: Molecular Instruments announced that its HCR (hybridization chain reaction) RNA-ISH technology is now being used to develop laboratory-developed tests (LDTs) for inflammatory skin disease diagnosis and stratification. This represents a shift from research-focused applications toward clinical diagnostic use, positioning HCR as a direct alternative to RNAscope in precision medicine workflows.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: Clinical LDTs represent a high-value market segment where ACD has built strong position with RNAscope validation and adoption. Molecular Instruments entering this space with HCR signals a strategic pivot by a direct technical competitor from academia and research into the regulated diagnostic market, where clinical accuracy, regulatory pathway clarity, and payer adoption are critical barriers. Success here would establish HCR as clinically credible and could accelerate MI's pursuit of other disease indications beyond skin.
at_risk: ACD's clinical LDT foothold in dermatology and other inflammation-driven indications if HCR demonstrates comparable performance and regulatory clearance
Opportunity to establish HCR as the standard-of-care ISH platform in precision diagnostics before RNAscope gains similar LDT traction in the same segments
Customer perception that HCR is a validated clinical alternative, especially among labs already familiar with MI from research use
holding_ground: RNAscope's deep, published validation across diverse tissue types and disease contexts provides clinical credibility that MI must still build for HCR in skin and beyond

ACD's platform-agnostic model allows customers to use RNAscope with their existing instruments and workflows, while HCR may require dedicated instrumentation or protocols that create switching friction

Single-molecule sensitivity and specificity of RNAscope are well-characterized in clinical contexts; HCR's performance in diagnostic-grade multiplexing and low-abundance transcripts in tissue sections remains less documented at scale

items.title: The barcode project: a strategy to track the early naïve reservoir
items.summary: PI: Una T O'Doherty. Institution: EMORY UNIVERSITY. Award: $775,512. Memory CD4+ T cells are believed to be the primary reservoir for HIV; however, emerging evidence suggests an important role for naïve CD4+ T cells. Specifically, sequencing studies suggest the naïve reservoir contributes substantially to the intact HIV reservoir in chronic progressors, but is practically absent in elite controllers. While the naïve reservoir is small, it may have outsized effects on the overall reservoir since we find the naïve reservoir repopulates the memory based on preliminary clone tracking studies. Objective: The long-term goal is to dissect the role of the naïve reservoir on the overall reservoir in acutely infected individuals and in a primary model of latency. We will first determine the extent and frequency of naïve infection in acutely treated individuals. The n
items.why: HIV reservoir tracking in CD4+ T cells; O'Doherty lab at Emory will need in situ validation of viral RNA and cell-type markers in lymph node tissue.

outreach_angle.hook: O'Doherty's team is tracking HIV-infected naïve vs. memory CD4+ T cells through sequencing and clone tracking, but will need to validate these findings directly in lymph node tissue where the reservoir resides. RNAscope enables them to spatially localize viral RNA and simultaneously identify CD4+ T cell subsets (naïve vs. memory markers) in intact tissue, bridging their molecular discovery back to tissue architecture.
outreach_angle.way_in: Sequencing tells them which clones are present and which cells are infected, but RNAscope answers the critical question: where in the lymph node are naïve vs. memory CD4+ T cells harboring HIV, and how do they relate spatially? This in situ validation is essential for understanding whether the naïve reservoir truly repopulates memory-based infection as their preliminary data suggests.
outreach_angle.routing: West territory (SEA) - Emory University, Atlanta. Infectious disease and immunology account manager should lead, with emphasis on multiplex viral RNA and immune cell phenotyping capability.

items.title: Court Finds That Two Advanced Cell Diagnostics Patents Are Not Infringed by Molecular Instruments’ Proprietary HCR™ RNA-ISH Technology
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: Court validates MI's HCR as non-infringing; clears path for MI to aggressively market against RNAscope without IP liability risk.

impact_brief.what_happened: A court has ruled that Molecular Instruments' HCR (hybridization chain reaction) RNA ISH technology does not infringe two ACD patents. This clears a significant legal barrier that could have constrained MI's ability to compete directly against RNAscope.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: MI is a direct, head-to-head competitor in RNA ISH with a differentiated technology platform. Removal of patent infringement risk eliminates a major friction point to their market expansion and sales messaging, allowing them to compete on performance and customer switching without legal encumbrance.
at_risk: Competitive intensity in RNA ISH market will likely increase as MI can now market and sell without IP litigation overhead or settlement risk.

holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and deep cross-tissue validation remain the technical foundation; this ruling changes legal risk, not performance.
ACD's platform-agnostic model (no instrument lock-in) is still a structural advantage against bundled competitors, and separates us from MI's technology specificity.
The ruling validates MI's tech but does not address customer switching costs, field support, assay portfolio maturity, or the breadth of RNAscope's market presence and reference data.

Near-term win rates for multiplex IF projects in academic and core facility segments where cycle time matters

items.title: Vizgen Founder Professor Xiaowei Zhuang Awarded 2026 Ernest Solvay Prize

Arnold, Jr. Professor of Science at Harvard University and Howard Hughes Medical... The post Vizgen Founder Professor Xiaowei Zhuang Awarded 2026 Ernest Solvay Prize appeared first on Vizgen .
items.why: Vizgen founder Zhuang wins Ernest Solvay Prize for MERSCOPE. Validates competitor's scientific credibility and platform momentum in spatial transcriptomics.

impact_brief.what_happened: Vizgen founder and Harvard professor Xiaowei Zhuang was awarded the 2026 Ernest Solvay Prize for her pioneering work in genome-scale imaging and spatial genomics, particularly the development of MERFISH technology underlying Vizgen's MERSCOPE platform. The award, presented by the Syensqo Fund, recognizes breakthrough discoveries in chemistry and positions Zhuang among elite scientists across multiple National Academies and major prizes.

Pharma/biotech mindshare: Decision makers in translational and clinical spaces may weight Vizgen's scientific foundation more heavily when evaluating spatial solutions for disease mapping and target discovery.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and specificity remain orthogonal strengths; MERFISH and RNAscope target overlapping but distinct use cases (transcriptome-scale vs. gene-focused), and awards do not change assay performance.

Platform independence is ACD's competitive moat: RNAscope works on any microscope and integrates into diverse workflows; Vizgen's ecosystem lock-in (MERSCOPE hardware, STARVUE software, InSituPlex assays) creates friction for customers who value flexibility.

Deep validation across tissue types and clinical applications: ACD's extensive RNAscope data package across pathology, oncology, and neuroscience is a practical asset that complements Vizgen's fundamental science narrative with real-world deployment evidence.

items.title: High resolution spatial transcriptomics decodes the microenvironmental determinants of response to Nr-CWS therapy in cervical precancerous lesions
items.summary: Immunotherapy with Nocardia rubra cell wall skeleton (Nr-CWS) can clear human papillomavirus (HPV) and induce regression of cervical precancerous lesions, yet many patients do not respond. The cellular and molecular basis for this heterogeneous clinical outcome remains unknown. Using high-resolution spatial transcriptomics, we profiled squamous intraepithelial lesions (SIL) from patients stratified by their subsequent response to Nr-CWS therapy. We discovered that non-responders were characterized by an immunosuppressive epithelial phenotype, defined by aberrant expression of the pro-inflammatory alarmin S100A9 in squamous epithelial cells (SECs), which was associated with poorer overall survival in cervical cancer. Non-responding lesions were enriched in proliferative (MKI67+) and pro-angiogenic (VEGFA+) SEC subpopulations and spatially organized into a pro-tumorigenic cellular neighborhood (CN-10) overexpressing S100A9 and ANXA1. In contrast, responders exhibited protective glandular-cell-enriched neighborhoods (CN-13) and supportive spatial interactions between lymphatic endothelial cells and fibroblasts. The unfavorable microenvironment was further defined by an immunosuppressi
items.why: High-resolution spatial transcriptomics study of cervical lesions and immunotherapy response; active TME/immuno-oncology lab validating epithelial and immune markers (S100A9, VEGFA, MKI67) in spatial context-strong RNAscope validation prospect.

items.title: Harnessing microglia-neuron communication to improve habituation in neurodevelopment disorders
items.summary: PI: Jose H Ledo. Institution: MEDICAL UNIVERSITY OF SOUTH CAROLINA. Award: $226,500. This research proposal explores the role of microglial cells in neurodevelopmental and neuropsychiatric disorders, particularly focusing on habituation deficits induced by maternal immune activation (MIA). Novelty habituation, a fundamental form of behavioral plasticity where the response to a repeated stimulus diminishes over time, is often disrupted in conditions like autism spectrum disorders (ASD). The underlying brain circuits and cellular mechanisms responsible for these deficits remain poorly understood, posing a significant barrier to developing effective therapeutic interventions. Our study hypothesizes that MIA, a model for prenatal infection, shifts microglial cells from a homeostatic to a reactive state, impairing their ability to regulate neuronal activity and thus disrupting
items.why: Newly funded neuro-psychiatric lab (autism, MIA model) validating microglia-neuron markers in brain tissue; RNAscope ideal for in situ circuit mapping.

outreach_angle.hook: This lab is mapping microglia-neuron communication circuits in autism and MIA models, which requires validating cell-type-specific markers and microglial activation states directly in tissue context. They need to confirm that their behavioral and cellular findings translate to specific regional and cellular expression patterns in intact brain.
outreach_angle.way_in: RNAscope lets them localize microglial activation markers (like Iba1, Il1b, Tmem119) and neuronal response genes simultaneously in the same tissue sections, avoiding the dissociation artifacts and antibody limitations that plague conventional immunofluorescence in inflamed tissue. items.title: TGF-β1-induced differentiation enhances chemotherapy response in metastatic colorectal cancer organoids
items.summary: BackgroundIn metastatic colorectal cancer, systemic therapies frequently fail, partly due to underlying phenotypic plasticity rooted in pre-existing multi-type cell populations. Intratumoral lineage hierarchies within colorectal tumors require renovated efforts to decode growth principles, design rational therapeutic approaches, and accurately interpret drug response. Understanding the cell-state dynamics of untreated tumors and the degree of cell responsiveness to exogenous stimuli is therefore crucial to improving currently underwhelming therapeutic outcomes. MethodsHere, we leveraged patient-derived organoids established from hepatic metastases of colorectal cancer patients to deconstruct population hierarchies by combining single-cell transcriptomics with single-molecule RNA fluorescent in situ hybridization. Computational frameworks were used to identify independent gene modules. We then employed flow cytometry analysis to track cytokine-induced population shifts using a cell surface marker, validating our findings through bulk RNA analysis, functional assays, and viability assays in response to oxaliplatin. ResultsOur data substantiate the existence of a dual population confi
items.why: Patient-derived colorectal cancer organoids combining scRNA-seq with smFISH for cell-state validation; active lab decoding tumor hierarchies and drug response.

items.title: Epigenetic de-repression of basal cell metaplasia in aging AT2 cells is a risk factor for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF).
items.summary: Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) is a fatal, age-associated lung disease in which alveolar type II (AT2) cells lose regenerative capacity and can adopt aberrant basal-like fates that promote fibrosis. Using 3D organoid co-cultures with primary human fibroblasts, we find that healthy human AT2 cell trans-differentiation into KRT5+/KRT17+ basal cells increases progressively with age, while differentiation into RAGE+ AT1-like cells decreases. We identify a shared gene signature in AT2 cells at downstream targets of p63 characterized both by acquisition of bivalent, poised chromatin marks with age and increased accessibility in IPF, indicating epigenetic ''priming'' towards a basal cell lineage. In vitro treatment of young AT2 cells with IL-1{beta} recapitulates this priming toward basal differentiation via a NF-kB-regulated histone demethylase, JMJD3. Conversion of primed AT2 cells to a basal fate requires recruitment of a shared transcription factor, KLF5, from AT1-specific to basal-specific promoters by HIF-1. AT2 cells instead convert to KRT5-/KRT17+ basaloid cells via a non-age-dependent pathway that requires KLF5-SMAD2/3 complexing through TGF{beta}1 signaling. These findings
items.why: IPF fibrosis lab validating KRT5, KRT17, RAGE, p63 targets in lung tissue; organoid work needs spatial confirmation of AT2-to-basal transition and marker colocalization.

items.title: Epithelial Reprogramming Underpinning Euplastic versus Dysplastic Repair
items.summary: PI: Jaymin J Kathiriya. Institution: ICAHN SCHOOL OF MEDICINE AT MOUNT SINAI. Award: $873,801. PROJECT SUMMARY Lung possesses a remarkable ability to regenerate and restore normal lung function after severe acute injury. However, in the case of chronic injuries, the lung epithelial stem/progenitors fail to restore normal lung function and contribute to a pathologic regeneration, amounting to regenerative failure that typifies chronic restrictive lung diseases like Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis (IPF). Both airway and alveolar epithelial stem cells are capable of differentiating into type 1 and type 2 alveolar epithelial cells (AT1 and AT2, respectively) via a series of intermediate cell states. During normal injury resolution, like in acute injuries in human lungs and murine models, both airway and alveolar epithelial stem cells go through a euplastic intermediate cell (EIC) state to
items.institution: ICAHN SCHOOL OF MEDICINE AT MOUNT SINAI
items.why: Lung epithelial stem cell differentiation and intermediate state validation in IPF; likely needs RNAscope for marker validation in tissue sections.

items.title: APOE interacts with COX-2 on lipid droplets to modulate inflammatory lipid signaling
items.summary: Alzheimer's Disease (AD) is the leading cause of dementia worldwide. Expression of the E4 variant of apolipoprotein E (APOE) greatly increases individuals' risk of developing AD. In response to lipogenesis in astrocytes, APOE can escape secretion and traffic to the cytoplasmic surface of lipid droplets (LDs), but protein interactors of APOE at the LD were unknown. Here we find that LD-localized APOE physically interacts with the inflammatory lipid signaling enzyme cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2). Like APOE, COX-2 can avoid the secretory pathway and traffic to LDs in response to lipogenesis. APOE3, but not APOE4, increases COX-2 localization to LDs. Computational modeling, microscopy-based assays, and targeted lipidomics reveal that APOE3 promotes while APOE4 suppresses COX-2 enzymatic activity at LDs and intracellular prostaglandin production. This work identifies a novel and targetable protein-protein interaction of APOE and provides a mechanistic link between APOE4 and dysregulated inflammatory lipid signaling.
items.why: AD neuroinflammation work in astrocytes needs APOE3/E4 and COX-2 subcellular localization validation; RNAscope validates mRNA trafficking to lipid droplets and glial subtype markers.

items.title: Blood-Brain Barrier Repair in Alzheimer’s Disease with Epilepsy
items.summary: PI: Bjoern Bauer, Anika M.S. Hartz. Institution: UNIVERSITY OF KENTUCKY. Award: $719,390. More than 25% of patients with Alzheimer’s disease (AD) develop epilepsy as co-morbidity. In AD with epilepsy (ADxEpi), seizures accelerate cognitive decline and further reduce life expectancy compared to AD alone. One hallmark of both AD and epilepsy is blood-brain barrier dysfunction. We discovered that barrier dysfunction is more severe in ADxEpi patients compared to seizure-free AD patients. Collectively, our data suggest that a combination of Ab and seizure-released glutamate (Aβ/Glu) triggers a dual positive feedback loop which exac- erbates barrier dysfunction, seizures, and cognitive decline in ADxEpi. items.why: BBB and neuroinflammation in AD+epilepsy; needs in situ validation of barrier proteins, Aβ, glia markers in human brain tissue.

They need to localize and validate barrier proteins, Aβ deposits, and neuroinflammatory markers (glia) in human brain tissue to confirm their dual positive feedback mechanism.
outreach_angle.way_in: RNAscope excels at detecting low-abundance transcripts and confirming protein-level findings in situ within intact tissue architecture. outreach_angle.routing: RBA East (Kentucky territory). Lead with neuroscience/neuroimmunology angle given BBB and glia focus.

items.title: Thyroid hormone mediated reprogramming of the basolateral amygdala by adolescent social deprivation
items.summary: PI: Deena M. Walker. Institution: OREGON HEALTH & SCIENCE UNIVERSITY. Award: $625,866. PROJECT SUMMARY Social disconnection in humans, particularly during adolescence, is associated with substance use disorder vulnerability. In mice, adolescent social isolation profoundly alters reward processing in adulthood; increasing the preference for drugs of abuse, enhancing associative learning, altering dopaminergic signaling and transcription within the BLA and extended reward circuitry. Intriguingly, these phenomena are all also associated with thyroid hormone signaling. Further, dysregulation of thyroid hormone in early life is associated lifetime risk for reward-related mood disorders and altered development of monoaminergic systems. In adults, thyroid hormone dysregulation is associated with substance- and alcohol use disorders. Therefore, we propose that the persistent impacts
items.why: Neuroscience lab studying adolescent social isolation, reward circuitry, and dopaminergic signaling in basolateral amygdala. Strong RNAscope prospect for validating transcriptional changes and receptor/neuropeptide localization in reward tissue.

They will need to validate cell-type-specific transcriptional changes and localize dopamine receptors, thyroid hormone receptors, and monoamine-related transcripts in intact tissue to connect their sequencing or bulk findings back to circuit function.

items.title: Dissecting Neuroglial Innate Immunity in vivo against Polyomavirus CNS Infection
items.summary: PI: Shareef Shaheen. Institution: PENNSYLVANIA STATE UNIV HERSHEY MED CTR. Award: $37,108. In immunocompromised individuals, JCPyV can cause progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML), an often-lethal demyelinating neurodegenerative disease. Because polyomaviruses (PyV) are species-specific, mechanisms of PML pathogenesis are incompletely understood. items.title: Development of novel NLRP3 inflammasome inhibitors for intervening in Alzheimer's disease
items.summary: PI: Shijun Zhang. Institution: VIRGINIA COMMONWEALTH UNIVERSITY. Award: $1,513,190. Neuroinflammation has been recognized as an essential player in the pathogenesis of Alzheimer’s disease (AD). Recently, the NOD-like receptor family pyrin domain containing 3 (NLRP3) inflammasome, a multimeric protein complex that tightly regulates the innate immune responses, has been suggested with critical roles in AD development and progression. Thus, NLRP3 inflammasome represents an attractive drug target for AD and offers promise to provide effective disease modifying potential. Over the past several years, our team has successfully developed novel small molecule NLRP3 inflammasome selective inhi
items.why: AD neuroinflammation lab with $1.5M grant will validate NLRP3, IL-1β, IL-18 targets in brain tissue; RNAscope needed for in situ marker confirmation.

items.title: A human lung organoid co-culture model of early bone sarcoma metastasis reveals contact-dependent epithelial remodeling at the metastatic interface
items.summary: Lung metastasis drives mortality across cancer types, yet how infiltrating tumor cells remodel the lung epithelium to establish metastatic niches remains poorly understood. Here we establish MESCUL (MEtastatic Sarcoma Co-CULture), a co-culture platform combining human lung organoids with patient-derived bone sarcoma cells to model early tumor-lung epithelial interactions in a physiologically relevant 3D system. MESCUL reveals that direct tumor-epithelial contact induces rapid, reproducible lung epithelial remodeling across Ewing sarcoma (ES) and osteosarcoma (OS) models and multiple organoid donor backgrounds, which is contact-dependent and not recapitulated by paracrine signaling. Single-cell RNA sequencing identifies LIMES (Lung Interface Metastasis Signature), a shared transcriptional program encompassing focal adhesion assembly, matrix metalloprotease (MMP) upregulation, and emergence of a damage-associated transitional cell state, in both ES and OS. Mechanistically, tumor-derived fibronectin (FN1) engages epithelial integrin receptors to activate focal adhesion kinase (FAK), driving amphiregulin (AREG) induction and MMP-mediated remodeling; FN1 alone phenocopies this response,
items.why: Lung organoid co-culture with sarcoma; scRNA-seq validates epithelial remodeling. RNAscope validates LIMES signature genes (MMP, adhesion markers) in situ within 3D model.

items.title: mTOR inhibition augments antitumor immune effector response by reprogramming the TP53-mutant, immune-cold HNSCC tumor microenvironment.
items.summary: Introduction: Resistance to immunotherapy remains a major clinical challenge in TP53-mutant head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC), a disease subset characterized by immune exclusion, high recurrence, and poor outcomes. Given the constitutive activation of PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling in TP53-mutant HNSCC and its role in disease progression, we investigated whether the mTOR inhibition (mTORi) could overcome immune resistance and improve outcomes. Methods: We evaluated the effects of mTOR inhibitor everolimus in TP53-mutant, anti-PD-1-resistant syngeneic HNSCC cell lines and syngeneic tumor models. Tumor microenvironment (TME) changes, including immune cell infiltration, immune checkpoint expression, and key pathways associated with immune suppression and angiogenesis were assessed to define the mechanisms underlying TME remodeling. Results: Everolimus significantly suppressed tumor growth in syngeneic HNSCC models. At the cellular level, everolimus significantly increased intratumoral CD8+ T cell and dendritic cell (DC) infiltration while reducing the accumulation of regulatory T cells (Tregs). Mechanistically, everolimus induced a cytokine/chemokine response, marked by increased
items.why: TP53-mutant HNSCC TME study with immune checkpoint and immune cell profiling; RNAscope ideal for validating PD-L1, immune markers, and checkpoint spatial distribution in syngeneic models.

items.title: Single-cell transcriptomics of heterogeneous patient-derived organoids reveals novel therapeutic targets in high-grade serous ovarian cancer
items.summary: High-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (HGSOC) is characterized by widespread peritoneal dissemination and poor long-term survival, largely driven by metastatic relapse following initial response to chemotherapy. Defining the molecular programs that enable tumor progression from the primary ovarian site to metastatic niches remains a key challenge. Here, we leverage patient-derived organoids (PDOs) coupled with single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) to interrogate tumor evolution and identify regulators of metastatic competence in HGSOC. We profiled PDOs and matched formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tumor samples from ovarian and omental disease sites across seven patients. Single-cell transcriptomic analysis revealed conserved and patient-specific cellular states and enabled reconstruction of inferred trajectories of tumor progression. Comparative trajectory analysis identified gene expression programs associated with metastatic transition from ovarian to omental tumors. Among these, the heparan sulfate proteoglycan AGRIN emerged as a consistently upregulated gene along the metastatic axis. items.title: Organization of Retinothalamic Circuits and their Contributions to Visual Processing in Primary Visual Cortex
items.summary: PI: EDWARD M CALLAWAY, Benjamin Knoell Stafford. Institution: SALK INSTITUTE FOR BIOLOGICAL STUDIES. Award: $475,000. Project Summary/Abstract The goal of this proposal is to characterize the connectivity and functional role of specific circuits in the mammalian visual system. This proposal focuses on neural circuits that transmit visual information from retinal ganglion cells (RGCs) in the retina, through different regions of the dorsal lateral geniculate nucleus (dLGN) in the thalamus, to specific populations of neurons in the primary visual cortex (V1). The combined activity of these visual pathway circuits results in the generation of tuning properties in V1 neurons (eg. direction selectivity) that make important contributions to visual perception and behavior. The objective of this proposal is to understand how specific retino-geniculo-cortical circuits, carrying different types of visual information
items.pi: EDWARD M CALLAWAY, Benjamin Knoell Stafford
items.why: Callaway lab (Salk) funded to map retino-geniculo-cortical circuits; needs marker validation in situ for RGC subtypes, thalamic relay, and V1 populations.

outreach_angle.hook: Callaway lab is mapping retino-geniculo-cortical circuits across retina, thalamus, and V1, which requires identifying and localizing specific neuronal populations (RGC subtypes, dLGN relay neurons, V1 cell types) that carry different visual information streams. This is a circuit neuroscientist persona who will need to validate cell-type markers and circuit connectivity in intact tissue.
outreach_angle.way_in: RNAscope allows them to visualize RNA markers for RGC subtypes, thalamic neurons, and V1 populations directly in tissue sections and intact circuits, confirming the anatomical localization of functionally defined cell types alongside their connectivity mapping and electrophysiology.
outreach_angle.routing: NCA territory, systems neuroscience/circuit mapping focus. Salk is established RNAscope user base.

items.title: Maintenance of mucosal homeostasis by commensal Th17 cells
items.summary: PI: Ivaylo Ivanov Ivanov. Institution: COLUMBIA UNIVERSITY HEALTH SCIENCES. Award: $567,000. Mucosa are sites of conventional immune system attack for defense against invasive pathogens. Mucosa were also once regarded simply as inert barriers preventing commensal microbes from accessing sterile tissues. Instead, emerging evidence suggests a complex interplay between commensal microbes, barrier epithelial cells, and the immune system that is essential for local and systemic homeostasis of the host. We have recently provided insight into how recognition of commensal components by epithelial and innate immune cells triggers a specific immune reaction and why the nature of this reaction could foster host- microbe symbiosis instead of outright microbial rejection. Specifically, we showed that antigen acquisition be intestinal epithelial cells (IECs) of commensal antigens drives a non-p
items.why: Ivanov lab (Columbia) studying mucosal Th17 and commensal immunity; gut barrier inflammation (profile 16) requires spatial validation of IL17, IFNG, cytokines in tissue.

outreach_angle.hook: Ivanov's lab is mapping how commensal-specific Th17 cells maintain gut mucosal homeostasis, which means validating cytokine production (IL-17, IFN-gamma) and T cell markers in intact tissue where spatial context matters. RNAscope fills a critical gap: cytokines like IL-17 lack reliable antibodies for immunohistochemistry, making in situ RNA hybridization the gold standard for localizing these signals in the lamina propria and epithelium.
outreach_angle.way_in: After flow sorting or single-cell sequencing, Ivanov's team will need to confirm which epithelial regions and immune compartments actually express IL-17 and downstream effectors in vivo. RNAscope multiplex assays let them simultaneously map Th17 activation markers, commensal-responsive genes, and barrier function transcripts on the same tissue section, bridging their molecular findings to histological anatomy.
outreach_angle.routing: NYA field rep; immunology/mucosal inflammation specialist angle.

items.title: Human 3D epithelioids enable continuous long-term clonal evolution studies across multiple epithelial tissues
items.summary: Modeling human epithelia in vitro remains challenging because current systems do not fully preserve the combination of architecture, heterogeneity, clonal composition, and long-term dynamics shown in vivo. While 3D approaches such as organoids and organotypic cultures capture important aspects of lineage differentiation and niche signaling, they often lose stable organization over time, limiting studies of long-lasting processes such as clonal evolution and cell competition. Here, we present human epithelioids as continuous long-term, 3D epithelial cultures efficiently derived from eight adult human epithelia, including trachea, skin, buccal mucosa, esophagus, blader, urethra, submandibular gland and endometrium. Using immunostaining, electron microscopy, single-cell RNA sequencing, functional assays and somatic mutation analyses, we deeply characterized human epithelioids and confirmed that they recapitulate native architecture and cell diversity, sustain regenerative capacity, and preserve donor-specific mutational landscapes, establishing a robust and versatile platform for longitudinal interrogation of clonal evolution, tissue dynamics and responses to clinically relevant pertu
items.why: 3D epithelioid models require marker validation in situ; RNAscope validates clonal diversity and cell-type identity across epithelial tissues.

items.title: AGBT: Spatial Biology Field ‘Maturing’ as Updates Well-Received, if Not Flashy
items.summary: Mar 04, 2026 | Andrew P. Han | Huanjia Zhang NEW YORK – New developments in spatial biology technology had a muted reception at this year’s Advances in Genome Biology and Technology general meeting, as... The post AGBT: Spatial Biology Field ‘Maturing’ as Updates Well-Received, if Not Flashy appeared first on Vizgen .
items.why: Vizgen conference coverage signals market maturation and competitor positioning; monitor MERSCOPE adoption trends and messaging shifts.

impact_brief.what_happened: At the AGBT conference in March 2026, Vizgen announced an on-instrument quality control assay for its MERSCOPE platform. The broader spatial biology field showed signs of maturation, with sequencing-based methods gaining ground and major competitors (10x Genomics, Akoya/Quanterix) pulling back from spotlight announcements, while Bruker, Stellaromics, Illumina, and others unveiled incremental platform updates.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: Vizgen's QC assay addresses a real pain point in sample economics, signaling that the spatial biology market is shifting from flashy capability announcements toward practical, workflow-integrated solutions that reduce customer friction and waste. This maturation means competitors are increasingly competing on usability and platform completeness rather than revolutionary new capabilities, and Vizgen is positioning MERSCOPE as a closed, integrated ecosystem where end-to-end QC reduces risk.
at_risk: Vizgen's on-instrument QC reinforces MERSCOPE as a turnkey platform and may increase lock-in appeal to labs seeking reduced sample failure and consolidated workflows, potentially eroding RNAscope's advantage as a modular, instrument-agnostic solution.

If QC capability becomes table-stakes in spatial biology, customers may perceive RNAscope's reliance on external validation and partner instruments as a friction point relative to integrated platforms.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and deep cross-tissue validation remain orthogonal strengths; QC assays do not change underlying detection specificity or the breadth of validated applications.

Platform modularity and independence from any single instrument ecosystem remain a genuine competitive advantage for labs wanting flexibility, especially those already invested in diverse imaging or sequencing hardware.

Vizgen's focus on QC suggests incremental, pragmatic competition rather than disruptive capability advances, meaning the field is validating established methods (including RNAscope) rather than rendering them obsolete.

items.title: Senescent and reactive astrocytes display distinct expression profiles and divergent functional capacities.
items.summary: Astrocytes assume multiple phenotypes in the brain in response to stress, injury, inflammation, and aging. Given the complexity of this critical cell type in the CNS, it is important to gain a greater understanding of the differences between these phenotypes and to potentially identify therapeutic approaches to modifying astrocyte function in the context of disease and aging. We compared senescent and reactive astrocytes using a strictly defined paradigm to induce these phenotypes in human astrocytes. Gene expression profiling reveals overlapping but distinct expression profiles. Reactive astrocytes predominantly express genes involved in inflammatory responses while senescent astrocytes express genes and a secretome that suggests a role in synaptic pruning. Unexpectedly, functional analysis in a simplified neurite outgrowth assay suggests that senescent astrocytes retain the ability to support neurite outgrowth while reactive astrocytes lose this capacity. The data suggests that senescent and reactive astrocytes play distinct functional roles in the physiology of the aging brain. However, the overlapping inflammatory nature of senescent and reactive astrocytes makes it difficult t
items.why: Astrocyte phenotyping (senescent vs reactive) in human CNS tissue validates markers GFAP, P2RY12, TMEM119 requiring in situ confirmation in neurodegeneration context.

items.title: scRNA-seq and genomics analyses reveal key mechanisms of inverted papilloma-associated sinonasal squamous cell carcinoma malignant transformation
items.summary: Sinonasal inverted papilloma (IP) carries a 10% risk of malignant transformation to IP-associated sinonasal squamous cell carcinoma (IP-SNSCC), yet the molecular and immune drivers of this progression remain poorly defined. This study integrates single-cell RNA sequencing, multiplexed spatial proteomics, whole-exome sequencing, and functional assays across IP and IP-SNSCC cohorts to define mechanisms of malignant transformation. Respiratory epithelial basal cells are identified as the putative cell of origin, with progression marked by recurrent CDKN2A loss and TP53 mutations. Spatial profiling reveals immune reorganization at the lesional interface in IP-SNSCC, characterized by enrichment of alternatively activated M2 macrophages. CXCL14 is shown to directly induce an immunosuppressive myeloid phenotype that suppresses T-cell IFN{gamma} production through an IDO-pathway-dependent mechanism. Integration of these multimodal datasets defines a previously unrecognized CXCL14-IDO mechanism that constrains anti-tumor immunity at the tumor-stroma interface. These findings establish IDO-targeted immunomodulation as a rational adjuvant strategy and provide a comprehensive molecular framewo
items.why: scRNA-seq + multiplexed spatial proteomics in sinonasal cancer; immune profiling (M2, CXCL14, IDO) suggests RNAscope validation need but proteomics-first design limits urgency.
